rulings.Case Title: JAVIER POYATOS VS. DAVID DUPREE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT DAVID DUPREE'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Set Aside Default. The motion is granted. Defendant is entitled to relief pursuant to CCP 473.5. The motion is timely, defendant provides a proposed answer, and defendant demonstrates a lack of actual notice. Mr. Dupree declares that he did not learn of this case until the insurer informed him of the judgment. rulings.Case Title: CRAIG E. REEDER VS. STATE BOARD OF PILOT COMMISSIONERS FOR THE BAYS OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 3, PETITIONER CRAIG REEDER'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate. Continued to August 7, 2015. It does not appear that Dept. 302 received a courtesy copy of the administrative record. Dept. 302 is in receipt of documents bates-labeled Reeder-90001 - Reeder-90043, filed with Petitioner's moving papers. However, Respondent refers to an administrative record in its opposition whereby the pages are numbered "AR." Dept. 302 is not in receipt of those documents. Petitioner must deliver courtesy copies of the administrative record to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 29, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SHARTSIS FRIESE LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 4, PETITIONER SHARTSIS FRIESE LLP'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied. The parties have a valid arbitration agreement and are scheduled to arbitrate the dispute on August 4, 2015. Accordingly, Plaintiff is entitled to provisional remedies, such as a writ of attachment, only if the requirements of CCP sec. 1281.8 are satisfied. Section 1281.8 provides that a provisional remedy may only be issued "only upon the ground that the award to which the applicant may be entitled may be rendered ineffectual without provisional relief." Plaintiff has not established that Defendant will be unable to pay any arbitration award that may be issued against him. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order and with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Of Ellen Stine And Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting Deposition Of Ellen Stine
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT SAKS & COMPANY's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Of Ellen Stine And Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting Deposition Of Ellen Stine Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to Appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to kathyhuibonhoa@gbgllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: AZAR AFSHARIPOUR VS. SAKS FIFTH AVENUE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SAKS & COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion is denied as to issues 1-3. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of the age and sex harassment and defendant's knowledge of the conduct that supports the claims. Whether the conduct was sufficiently severe or pervasive to constitute harassment is a factual issue. See Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2006) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 264. Plaintiff's and Ms. Wald's declarations show that Mr. Nikolaev routinely verbally and physically assaulted both of them and plaintiff witnessed some of the mistreatment of Ms. Wald. The court grants summary adjudication as to issue 4. Plaintiff fails to present evidence of an adverse employment action that supports the retaliation claim. The single formal discipline of plaintiff and the litigation hold memorandum are insufficient as a matter of law to constitute an adverse employment action. Those acts did not materially alter the terms or conditions of plaintiff's employment. In Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1060, the employer engaged in a pattern of systematic retaliation. The court denies summary adjudication as to issue 5. Plaintiff's and Ms. Wald's declarations show that defendant had sufficient notice of the misconduct and it did not respond in an appropriate manner to support the failure to prevent cause of action. The court denies summary adjudication as to issue 6. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. At most, defendant has shown that Mr. Hagman was not a managing agent. Defendant fails, however, to demonstrate that any of the other employees of defendant that had notice of Mr. Nikolaev's conduct and/or had a role in his discipline were not managing agents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY PAROLARI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's second cause of action for Willful Misconduct and Neglect is sustained without leave to amend. The facts set forth in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") allege a cause of action for professional negligence. The Elder/Dependent Adult Abuse Act "excludes liability for professional negligence." (Sababin v. Superior Court (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 81, 88; see also Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 781.) rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY PAROLARI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike punitive damages and attorneys' fees is granted. Plaintiff cannot recover punitive damages on her negligence claim, as Plaintiff does not have a Court order permitting her to claim punitive damages, as required by CCP sec. 425.13. Defendant's motion to strike prejudgment interest is denied. Under Civil Code § 3287(a) "[a] person who is entitled to recover damages certain, or capable of being made certain by calculation, and the right to recover which is vested in the person upon a particular day, is entitled also to recover interest thereon from that day, except when the debtor is prevented by law, or by the act of the creditor from paying the debt¿" Here, Plaintiff's damages are capable of being made certain by calculation because she is claiming loss of income and decedent's annual income is calculable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)
